name: El Ministerio del tiempo VR: Salva el tiempo

reviews.review_description: FYI- This experience is in Spanish OR English.  I must say that this is exactly what VR should be!!!!! This is in my top 5 VR apps now (Vtime, GrooVR, Dead Secret and Land's End). It's immersive, a little challenging, exciting and works smoothly without technical difficulties. My only issue is that it's short - give us MORE, MORE, MORE! please.
Best visual vr game!! Full immersion in a great experience in 360 3D ambient!
Nice mini-adventure with real actors and thrilling action. Some voices were not dubbed into English though. Also, it was not clear to me why I had to fix that radio set.

reviews.review_title: Great experience,  would love full game
reviews.review_description: Greay job of creating tense drama. This is a very very well done free game / experience. The graphics and atmosphere are great, and the audio is nice as well. I like the mix of gameplay and video. In the second room, the woman just kept saying "I think there's a part missing" over and over again, which was pretty annoying and unnecessary. Other than that, really nice app and I wish there was a full version of this experience

First of all, if I ever play this again, the nagging man and woman in the search areas will be stifled.  I never did find out what the flashlight was for??? 
Cool game though. Finished with 10 seconds to spare without realizing exactly what I did. I will go back and turn the sound down. 
Hope for more of this!

reviews.review_title: Long on Effort, Short on Time
reviews.review_description:
You're running out of time - that's definitely the defining feature of this game. As a narrative device, a ticking clock is a way to add tension and excitement to a scene. However, in gameplay, adding a timer just makes an otherwise interesting mission or puzzle seem that much more frustrating.

The actual gameplay, trying to solve puzzles with physical objects around the room, is rather interesting. It gives a kind of "escape room" vibe as you're looking around for clues or puzzle pieces. It uses a teleport-travel mechanic, which I will always hate, movement isn't key in these small rooms and that's more a personal gripe, so I won't dock points for that. However, the timer is WAY too short. Five minutes seems like a long amount of time, but in the first puzzle there are four clues to find, meaning you have just over a minute for each solution.

As well as the gameplay, running out of time is also the story - everyone in the Ministry of Time is rushing to solve some temporal issue... which seems odd. Doesn't time travel mean that you have as much time as you want to do anything? There's some 3D/Panoramic full-motion video on display, and the actors were pretty good for a videogame - they were believable, if a little campy (although the voices were dubbed into English, which feels weird in VR). But, it actually felt weird to have put all of that effort into a game with only 2 missions.
Now that I think about it, each mission had a five-minute timer, that's 10 minutes of gameplay, so I think there's actually more cutscene than gameplay in this game...

This experience ultimately gave me more enjoyment than frustration, but I still exited the game feeling unfulfilled. Worth the price tag, but not the time.
Great concept and very immersive experience.
